title: Cerasus wuyiensis
intro: Cerasus wuyiensis, also known as Wuyi cherry or mountain apricot, is a species of deciduous shrub or small tree in the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Cerasus wuyiensis is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is also used to make infusions, extracts, and dried flowers for medicinal purposes.
taxonomy: Cerasus wuyiensis is classified as a species within the genus Cerasus, which is a group of deciduous shrubs and small trees commonly known as cherry plants. This genus is further grouped into the family Rosaceae, which consists of a wide variety of flowering plants, including roses, apricots, and plums. The family Rosaceae is part of the larger order Rosales, which includes other families of flowering plants. Cerasus wuyiensis is native to China and is considered a rare species. Its classification helps botanists understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
uses: Cerasus wuyiensis, a species of cherry, has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits.
